messages.content:
Normalize descriptor tokens for clean chip rendering

## Summary
Extracts descriptor normalization logic into a reusable utility function and applies it to the work registry page. This ensures descriptors are rendered as clean, individually-delimited chips rather than raw comma-joined strings.

## Changes
- **New utility:** `src/lib/ledger/descriptors.ts`
  - Adds `normalizeDescriptors()` function that:
    - Accepts any input and safely returns an empty array if not an array of strings
    - Splits each element on commas and trims whitespace
    - Drops empty tokens
    - De-duplicates while preserving first-seen order (stable for React keys)
    - Mirrors the `declare_volley` RPC merge behavior (Phase 3 polish)

- **Updated:** `src/app/registry/[id]/page.tsx`
  - Replaces inline descriptor type-checking with call to `normalizeDescriptors()`
  - Improves code clarity with explanatory comment linking to the RPC behavior
  - Ensures legacy run-on comma-joined values render as separate chips instead of one long string

## Implementation Details
The normalization function handles edge cases gracefully:
- Non-array inputs return `[]` (safe fallback)
- Non-string elements are skipped
- Duplicate tokens are never added (prevents React key collisions)
- Order is preserved by tracking seen tokens in a Set while building the output array

https://claude.ai/code/session_011JXZo5qchpy3cqEiWXDehG

Add Go Live action to publish a draft work (Phase 4)

Owner-only draft -> live flip on /registry/[id]:
- goLive(workId) server action runs as the user (session-bound Supabase
  client, not the service role); RLS work_owner_upd enforces ownership and
  a status='draft' guard makes a re-click a harmless no-op once live.
- <GoLiveButton> renders only for the owner of a draft, confirms intent
  ("Make this public?"), then calls the action; the guard hides it once live.

Red Line player, ledger trail, and editor untouched. One-directional for
now (draft -> live).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KQCbiuzA4DwdqJVej4VDrF

Phase 4 polish: anchor synced lyrics in their own scroll box

Two scroll containment bugs on AIRED-0001 — highlight and timing were
correct; only the page-scroll behaviour was off. Fix both so the line the
user is watching (armed / lit) stays put and on-screen while only the
surrounding lyrics move inside their own box.

* Tap-sync editor: pin the transport bar (Play/Pause · Restart · seek ·
  Tap · Back-one-line · armed indicator) just under the site header so
  long lyrics no longer push the Tap control out of view. The line list
  scrolls inside its own bounded `max-h-96 overflow-y-auto` container
  with `overscroll-contain`, never by growing the page. A new effect
  re-centers the armed line in that container every time it advances or
  is re-armed, honoring prefers-reduced-motion (instant under reduce,
  smooth otherwise). Spacebar tap is untouched.

* Public synced-lyrics: switch the centering math to
  `getBoundingClientRect` so the active line is anchored to the
  container's center regardless of offsetParent — the previous offsetTop
  read could resolve against the document and send `scrollTo` past the
  container's own range. Add `relative` + `overscroll-contain` so all
  motion stays inside the lyrics box and never scrolls the page.

LRC parsing, saveLyrics, RLS, the migration, and RedLinePlayer's audio
internals are all untouched.

Post-door fixes: default to / after login + render the Google G

Two small follow-ups on top of the listener's-door work:

- safeNext's fallback was still "/registry", so any caller that didn't
  pass an explicit default (the OAuth callback at /auth/callback and the
  email token route at /auth/confirm) would land a plain login on the
  old list page. Change the helper default to "/" so every consumer
  arrives at the listener's home; signIn / signUp / signInWithGoogle
  already pass DEFAULT_NEXT = "/" so the explicit-next behaviour (Create
  → /upload) is unchanged.

- The inline Google "G" mark on the auth buttons used viewBox 0 0 24 24
  while its path coordinates extend to ~44, so the whole logo rendered
  off-canvas (an empty 16×16 box on /login and /signup). Correct the
  viewBox to 0 0 48 48 so the standard 4-color mark actually shows.
  Sign-in behaviour is unchanged; it's only the icon.

Phase 3 auto-trigger: kick the transcode worker on createWork

A fresh upload now transcodes itself. After `createWork()` inserts the
work row, `after(() => triggerTranscode(workId))` POSTs the existing
Railway worker (auth + body shape exactly as worker/src/index.js checks:
`Authorization: Bearer <secret>` + `{ work_id }`), so the user redirects
to the draft page while ffmpeg + R2 upload run in the background and
fill `audio_master_key` / `hls_playlist_key` on their own. Status stays
`draft` — Go Live remains a deliberate click.

The fetch uses a 5s AbortController so the Vercel function doesn't sit
waiting for the full transcode; an AbortError after the worker has read
the body is the expected path and does not cancel the in-flight job.
Missing env vars (AIRED_WORKER_URL / TRANSCODE_SHARED_SECRET) log a
skip and the manual curl form still works as the backfill path.

Uniform work cards: cap chips, fix row heights, +N overflow

A 2-contributor card and a 7-contributor card now render at the same
height. The chip area is a single fixed-height row capped at 3 visible
chips; any extras fold into a compact "+N" indicator instead of
wrapping to new lines that grow the card. Long chip names truncate
with ellipsis at max-w-[7rem]. Title and duration rows are also
fixed-height. Chips stay <Link>s to /agent/[slug] and the Red Line
badge logic is unchanged.

Dedupe of repeated contributors already lives at the data layer
(src/lib/works/queries.ts:shape), so a contributor that appears in
multiple volleys renders as a single chip — work #3's card now shows
three chips, not four.

Reuse existing contributors on upload, don't fork the discography

Adding a contributor that already exists (e.g. "AISong.org") now resolves
to the canonical agent row instead of inserting a duplicate. Match is
case-insensitive on the trimmed name, plus a slug check that catches
punctuation/whitespace collisions ("AISong.org" → "aisong-org"). A
partial unique index backstops the app-level find-or-create against
concurrent inserts; the existing "Tee Momtaz" orphan is grandfathered
by slug so existing data is untouched.

Add repeat mode (off/all/one) to the player

The session queue stopped dead after the last track. This adds a repeat mode so the radio can keep rolling (the Phase 5 "radio plays continuously" foundation).

## PlayerProvider
- New `repeatMode` state (`"off" | "all" | "one"`, default `"off"`) and a `cycleRepeatMode` action that walks **off → all → one → off**. The mode is mirrored into a ref so the stable transport callbacks read it without being re-created.
- The `ended` handler now branches:
  - **off** → hands off to `next()`, which stops at the last track (`isPlaying=false`) — unchanged behavior.
  - **all** → `next()` wraps to index 0 and keeps playing.
  - **one** → replays the current track in place (seek 0 + play), no advance.
- `next()` reworked to compute a target index: the next track, or under **all** a wrap to 0, or stop under **off** at the last track. A single-track **all** queue restarts in place (the index doesn't change, so the source won't re-attach on its own). Manual ⏭ on the last track wraps under **all**; the OS media-session "next" control inherits this too.

## now-playing-bar
- Repeat toggle beside prev/play/next: **off** = idle/muted, **all** = active (cert-red), **one** = active with a small "1" badge.
- ⏭ stays enabled on the last track when repeat-all so it can wrap.

## Verification
- `tsc --noEmit`, `eslint`, and a full `next build` all pass clean.
- Traced each case by hand: repeat-all last-track-end → first plays; repeat-one → same track loops; off → stops at end; manual ⏭ wraps under all; single-track-all restarts correctly.

_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code/session_01FgLopzq4ukhDfb1E9oizTv)_

Top nav: highlight the current page, not just the red CTA

## Problem

The active page was dimmed like every other nav link while the red Upload button pulled the eye as if it were the destination — so people tapped **Listen** just to confirm where they were.

## Change (styling only — no routing or item changes)

- **New `src/components/nav/nav-link.tsx`** (`"use client"`): a small client island that reads `usePathname()` and computes active state. `SiteHeader` stays a server component (it still awaits `getCurrentUser()`). Match rule: exact for `/`, `startsWith` for `/upload`.
- **Active text link** (`Listen` on `/`): `text-foreground` + a cert-red `after:` underline bar (the Red Line as a "you are here" mark).
- **Inactive**: stays `text-muted hover:text-foreground` — brightens on hover.
- **Active CTA** (`Upload` on `/upload`): keeps its `bg-cert-red`, but gains an active ring (`ring-2 ring-white/70 ring-offset-2 ring-offset-background`) so it reads as *current*, not merely *primary*.
- Adds `aria-current="page"` on the active item.

All four nav items are kept; only the active/hover treatment changed.

## Verification

- `tsc --noEmit`: clean · `eslint`: clean · `next build`: exit 0 (all 20 pages generated, incl. `/` and `/upload`).
- `usePathname` usage confirmed against the installed Next.js 16.2.9 docs; no `rewrites`/`cacheComponents` and the proxy only refreshes the auth cookie, so no hydration-mismatch risk.

On `/`, `Listen` is bright with a red underline; on `/upload`, `Upload` carries the active ring while `Listen` drops to muted; hovering a non-active item brightens it.



https://claude.ai/code/session_012ppnEG51k2Gd6Xc8BSiABc

Add contact email link to site footer

## Summary
Added a mailto contact link to the site footer navigation, allowing users to easily reach out via email.

## Changes
- Added a new contact link (`mailto:contact@ai-red.io`) in the footer navigation
- Link uses consistent styling with other footer links (transition + hover effect)
- Positioned between the Covenant link and IntroReplayLink component

## Implementation Details
- Uses a standard HTML `` tag with `href="mailto:"` for email functionality
- Applies the same `transition hover:text-foreground` classes as adjacent footer links for visual consistency
- No changes to component logic or data model

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CovGkrbHffgk3r98VrYxsu

diff --git a/src/components/site-footer.tsx b/src/components/site-footer.tsx index bd3e922..86c1d0c 100644 --- a/src/components/site-footer.tsx +++ b/src/components/site-footer.tsx @@ -34,6 +34,12 @@ export function SiteFooter() { Covenant + + Contact + Σ I · {year}

Add Vercel Web Analytics with app install tracking

## Summary
Integrate Vercel Web Analytics into the AIRED platform to track page views and custom events, with initial instrumentation for app installation tracking.

## Changes
- **Added Vercel Analytics dependency** (`@vercel/analytics@^2.0.1`) to `package.json`
- **Initialized Analytics component** in root layout (`src/app/layout.tsx`):
  - Imported and rendered `` from `@vercel/analytics/next`
  - Added explanatory comment noting that the component is privacy-friendly and inert until enabled in the Vercel dashboard
- **Instrumented app install event** in `src/components/install/install-coach.tsx`:
  - Imported `track` function from `@vercel/analytics`
  - Added `track("app_installed")` call in the `onInstalled` handler
  - Included detailed comment explaining that the event fires once per successful add-to-home-screen and is counted exactly once per install, with no PII in the payload

## Implementation Details
- The Analytics component renders nothing and only injects the collector script; data collection is gated by Vercel dashboard configuration
- App install tracking leverages the browser's native `appinstalled` event, which fires once per successful installation (Android prompt acceptance or iOS/desktop manual install)
- InstallCoach mounts once in the layout, ensuring each install is counted exactly once
- Event name only (`app_installed`) with no sensitive data in the payload, maintaining privacy standards

https://claude.ai/code/session_018qBEJmGbrfnRWh93NjLyEx

Fix WorkTitle sm layout: stack ID and title, increase card height

## Summary
Refactored the `WorkTitle` component's `sm` size variant to use a stacked vertical layout that prevents long titles from being silently clipped in narrow cards. Also increased the work card's height to accommodate the new layout.

## Changes
- **WorkTitle component (`work-title.tsx`)**
  - Extracted `sm` size into its own early-return branch with a dedicated vertical flex layout
  - ID now displays on its own line in smaller, uppercase mono text with the separator bullet
  - Title displays below with `line-clamp-2` and `break-words` to ensure long titles wrap and show ellipsis rather than overflow
  - Removed `sm` case handling from the shared `md`/`lg` logic paths for clarity
  - Updated component comment to document the stacking behavior at `sm` breakpoint

- **WorkCard component (`work-card.tsx`)**
  - Increased WorkTitle link container height from `h-10` (40px) to `h-[3.75rem]` (60px) to accommodate the two-line title layout

## Implementation Details
The `sm` layout now uses:
- `flex flex-col gap-y-0.5` for vertical stacking with minimal gap
- `min-w-0` on the container to enable proper text wrapping in flex children
- `line-clamp-2` on the title to limit to 2 lines with ellipsis
- `break-words` to ensure long unbroken strings wrap correctly

This ensures titles in narrow cards (e.g., mobile work cards) never get silently clipped and always show the ellipsis indicator when truncated.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PxhrmkGgx9WgeDTwatur2q
